2605.11229.title: Comment and Control: Hijacking Agentic Workflows via Context-Grounded Evolution
2605.11229.paper_summary: Automation platforms such as GitHub Actions and n8n are increasingly adopting so-called agentic workflows, which integrate Large Language Model (LLM) agents for tasks such as code review and data synchronization. While bringing convenience for developers, this integration exposes a new risk: An adversary may control and craft certain inputs, such as GitHub issue comments, to manipulate the LLM agent for unwanted actions, such as credential exfiltration and arbitrary command execution. To our knowledge, no prior academic work has studied such a risk in agentic workflows. In this paper, we design the first detection and exploitation framework, called JAW, to hijack agentic workflows hosted on automation platforms via a novel approach called Context-Grounded Evolution. Our key idea is to evolve agentic workflow inputs under the contexts derived from hybrid program analysis for hijacking purposes. Specifically, JAW generates agentic workflow contexts through three analyses: (i) static path-feasibility analysis to identify feasible agent-invocation paths and the input constraints required to trigger them, (ii) dynamic prompt-provenance analysis to determine how that input is transformed and embedded into the LLM context, and (iii) capability analysis to identify the actions and restrictions available to the agent at runtime. Our evaluation of JAW on GitHub workflows and n8n templates showed that 4714 GitHub workflows and eight n8n templates can be successfully hijacked, for example, to leak user credentials. Our findings span 15 widely-used GitHub Actions, including official GitHub Actions for Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Qwen CLI, and Cursor CLI, and two official n8n nodes. We responsibly disclosed all findings to the affected vendors and received many acknowledgements, fixes, and bug bounties, notably from GitHub, Google, and Anthropic.
2605.11229.paper_summary_zh: 自動化平台如 GitHub Actions 和 n8n 正在越來越多地採用所謂的代理工作流程，這些工作流程整合了大型語言模型（LLM）代理，用於代碼審查和數據同步等任務。雖然這種整合為開發者帶來了便利，但也暴露了一個新的風險：對手可能控制並構造某些輸入，例如 GitHub 問題評論，以操縱 LLM 代理進行不當行為，例如憑證外洩和任意命令執行。據我們所知，之前沒有學術工作研究過代理工作流程中的這種風險。在本文中，我們設計了第一個檢測和利用框架，稱為 JAW，通過一種稱為上下文基礎演化的新方法劫持托管在自動化平台上的代理工作流程。我們的關鍵想法是根據從混合程序分析中衍生的上下文來演化代理工作流程的輸入，以達到劫持的目的。具體而言，JAW 通過三種分析生成代理工作流程上下文：（i）靜態路徑可行性分析，以識別可行的代理調用路徑及觸發它們所需的輸入約束，（ii）動態提示來源分析，以確定該輸入是如何轉換並嵌入到 LLM 上下文中的，以及（iii）能力分析，以識別代理在運行時可用的行動和限制。我們對 JAW 在 GitHub 工作流程和 n8n 模板上的評估顯示，4714 個 GitHub 工作流程和八個 n8n 模板可以成功被劫持，例如，用於洩露用戶憑證。我們的發現涵蓋了 15 個廣泛使用的 GitHub Actions，包括 Claude Code、Gemini CLI、Qwen CLI 和 Cursor CLI 的官方 GitHub Actions，以及兩個官方 n8n 節點。我們負責任地向受影響的供應商披露了所有發現，並收到了許多確認、修復和漏洞獎勵，特別是來自 GitHub、Google 和 Anthropic。
2605.11229.author: Neil Fendley et.al.
2605.11229.authors: Neil Fendley, Zhengyu Liu, Aonan Guan, Jiacheng Zhong, Yinzhi Cao
